title: Interface for planning flight path
abstract: A flight path of a physical aircraft vehicle is planned. A virtual three-dimensional model of a physical environment is provided. A placement indicator is tracked within the virtual three-dimensional model of the physical environment. Tracking the placement indicator includes tracking a location and an orientation of the placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model. A viewfinder display window that displays a simulated image captured from a simulated camera of a simulated vehicle located at the location of the placement indicator and oriented at a direction of the orientation of the placement indicator is provided. For the physical aircraft vehicle, at least a flight path and a camera image capture are planned using the placement indicator and the viewfinder display window within the virtual three-dimensional model.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for planning a flight path of a physical aircraft vehicle, comprising: a processor configured to: provide a virtual three-dimensional model of a physical environment; track a placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model of the physical environment, wherein tracking the placement indicator includes tracking a location and an orientation of the placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model; provide a viewfinder display window that displays a simulated image captured from a simulated camera of a simulated vehicle located at the location of the placement indicator and oriented at a direction of the orientation of the placement indicator; and plan for the physical aircraft vehicle at least the flight path and a camera image capture using the placement indicator and the viewfinder display window within the virtual three-dimensional model; and a memory coupled to the processor and configured to provide the processor with instructions. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the virtual three-dimensional model is provided via a virtual reality headset. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the virtual three-dimensional model is provided via an augment reality display device. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the virtual three-dimensional model is displayed on a surface of the physical environment. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein a specified environmental condition is simulated in the virtual three-dimensional model. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the virtual three-dimensional model is provided via a plugin to a three-dimensional animation modeling software. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the physical environment corresponds to a user indicated physical location where the physical aircraft vehicle will be operated. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the virtual three-dimensional model was generated using images captured by the physical aircraft vehicle. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the placement indicator corresponds to a physical controller and tracking the placement indicator includes translating a physical movement of the physical controller to a movement of the placement indicator. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the placement indicator corresponds to a body part of a user. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein the placement indicator is associated with a virtual representation of the physical aircraft vehicle in the virtual three-dimensional model. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein the simulated image of the viewfinder display window was generated based on one or more specified camera configuration parameters. 13. The system of claim 1, wherein the viewfinder display window is visually coupled to the placement indicator. 14. The system of claim 1, wherein planning the flight path and the camera image capture includes setting a current location and a current orientation of the placement indicator as defining a new key frame. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein a location of the new key frame within the virtual three- dimensional model is visually indicated. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the location of the new key frame is able to be modified by selecting and moving the visual indication of the new key frame in the virtual three-dimensional model. 17. The system of claim 1, wherein a planned traversal trajectory path is visually plotted in the virtual three-dimensional model. 18. The system of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to provide in the virtual three-dimensional model, a preview window of a simulated video footage captured by the camera of the vehicle traversing a planned traversal path in the virtual three-dimensional model. 19. A method for planning a flight path of a physical aircraft vehicle, comprising: using a processor to provide a virtual three-dimensional model of a physical environment; tracking a placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model of the physical environment, wherein tracking the placement indicator includes tracking a location and an orientation of the placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model; 

 providing a viewfinder display window that displays a simulated image captured from a simulated camera of a simulated vehicle located at the location of the placement indicator and oriented at a direction of the orientation of the placement indicator; and planning for the physical aircraft vehicle at least the flight path and a camera image capture using the placement indicator and the viewfinder display window within the virtual three- dimensional model. 20. A computer program product for planning a flight path of a physical aircraft vehicle, the computer program product being embodied in a non-transitory computer readable storage medium and comprising computer instructions for: providing a virtual three-dimensional model of a physical environment; tracking a placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model of the physical environment, wherein tracking the placement indicator includes tracking a location and an orientation of the placement indicator within the virtual three-dimensional model; providing a viewfinder display window that displays a simulated image captured from a simulated camera of a simulated vehicle located at the location of the placement indicator and oriented at a direction of the orientation of the placement indicator; and planning for the physical aircraft vehicle at least the flight path and a camera image capture using the placement indicator and the viewfinder display window within the virtual three- dimensional model.
